response:
"What would an innocent person say after he falsely received a verdict from the court?"

SECTION 1  
An innocent person might feel a mix of confusion and disbelief upon receiving a verdict they did not deserve. The weight of the moment could lead to a flood of emotions, leaving them speechless. In that moment, they might think of the silver lining that still exists in their life, even in the face of injustice. The courtroom, once a place of hope, now feels like a prison of uncertainty. A stranger in the crowd might catch their eye, offering a silent gesture of support that brings a fleeting sense of comfort.

SECTION 2  
They might say, "I didn’t do this, and I never will," as a way to assert their innocence. The words could feel heavy, like a lead weight on their chest, but they would speak them with conviction. Sir, I ask you to believe in me, for I have nothing to hide. The truth is out there, waiting to be uncovered, and I will not let this injustice define me. Wrap your arms around yourself, for this is not the end of your story.
